title: Crispy Spicy Kenyan Borlotti Beans Fritters
description: A fiery, crunchy Kenyan snack that marries tender borlotti beans with a spiced chickpea batter, finished with a fermented soy tang for depth and fresh coriander for brightness.

ingredients.name: fermented soy sauce (tamari)
ingredients.notes: adds umami and fermented flavor, dairy‑free

instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine the borlotti beans, chickpea flour, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, salt, and chopped chili, stirring to coat the beans evenly.
Gradually whisk in the warm water, forming a thick but pourable batter that clings to the beans; fold in the fermented soy sauce.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium‑high heat; test by dropping a small amount of batter – it should sizzle immediately.
Drop spoonfuls of batter into the hot oil, flattening gently with the back of a spoon; fry 3–4 minutes per side until each fritter is golden and crisp, being careful not to overcrowd the pan.
Lift the fritters with a slotted spoon, drain on paper towels, and keep warm.
Sprinkle the chopped fresh coriander over the hot fritters for a burst of brightness.
Serve immediately with a squeeze of lime and a side of cool cucumber salad if desired.
For extra heat, add a pinch of cayenne or serve with a garlic‑chili dip on the side.
